description: 2001 attacks in the United States
description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

aliases: National National Parks ServicePark Service, NPS
National United States Parks ServicePark Service of the United States
description: United States federal agency
description_wiki: The National Park Service (NPS) is an agency of the United States federal government, within the United States Department of the Interior.

description: federal district of the United States of America, containing the capital city of the United States, Washington
geographic region of Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Northern Virginia, locally referred to as NOVA or NoVA, comprises several counties and independent cities in the Commonwealth of Virginia in the United States.

description: United States Navy base in Washington, DC, United States
description_wiki: Naval Support Facility (NSF) Anacostia is  a United States Naval Base in Washington, D.C., close to where the Anacostia River joins the Potomac River.

description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: U.S. federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) is the domestic intelligence and security service of the United States and its principal federal law enforcement agency.

description: U.S. federal government agency
description_wiki: The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) is an independent agency of the United States government tasked with environmental protection matters.

description: The United States Department of War was a cabinet department that managed and oversaw the United States Army.
description_wiki: The United States Department of Defense (DOD), also referred to as the Department of War (DOW), is an executive department of the United States federal government charged with coordinating and supervising the United States Armed Forces—the Army, Navy, Marines, Air Force, Space Force, and, for some purposes, the Coast Guard—and related functions and agencies.

description: criminal investigative arm of the Office of the Inspector General, U.S. Department of Defense
description_wiki: The Defense Criminal Investigative Service (DCIS) is the criminal investigative arm of the United States Department of Defense Office of Inspector General.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

description: tributary of the Potomac River in Maryland and Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The Anacostia River  is a river in the Mid-Atlantic region of the United States.

description: subregion of the United States National Park Service's National Capital Region
memorial in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Lincoln Memorial is  a U.S. national memorial honoring Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the United States, located on the western end of the National Mall of Washington, D.C. The memorial is built in a neoclassical style in the form of a classical temple.

description: United States Air Force facility in Virginia, USA
description_wiki: Langley Air Force Base (IATA: LFI, ICAO: KLFI, FAA LID: LFI) is a United States Air Force base located in Hampton, Virginia, adjacent to Newport News.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.

description: uniformed federal law enforcement agency
description_wiki: The United States Park Police (USPP) is the oldest uniformed federal law enforcement agency in the United States.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Maryland (US:  ) is a state in the Mid-Atlantic and Southeastern regions of the United States.

surface_forms: George Washington Parkway under Superintendent Audrey Calhoun's direction
description: 7,142-acre parkway in Virginia and the District of Columbia in the United States, that is maintained by the National Park Service
national foreign intelligence agency of the United States
description_wiki: The Central Intelligence Agency (CIA)  is a civilian foreign intelligence service of the federal government of the United States tasked with advancing national security through collecting and analyzing intelligence from around the world and conducting covert operations.

description: Potomac River crossing between the states of Maryland and Virginia, United States
auxiliary Interstate Highway in Washington, D.C. metropolitan areas of Maryland and Virginia in the United States
island in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: Lady Bird Johnson Park, formerly known as Columbia Island until 1968, is an island located in the Potomac River in Washington, D.C., in the United States.

description: historic district and National Historical Park Gillespie County and Johnson City in Texas, United States
description_wiki: Lyndon B. Johnson National Historical Park is a United States National Historical Park in central Texas about 50 miles (80 km) west of Austin in the Texas Hill Country.

description: official residence and office of the President of the United States
description_wiki: The White House is the official residence and workplace of the President of the United States.

description: historical area from the American Civil War
description_wiki: Antietam National Battlefield is a National Park Service-protected area along Antietam Creek in Sharpsburg, Washington County, northwestern Maryland.

description: memorializing the Battle of Monocacy (1864), fought near the Monocacy River
description_wiki: Monocacy National Battlefield is a unit of the National Park Service, the site of the Battle of Monocacy in the American Civil War fought on July 9, 1864.

description: 2,500 acres at confluence of Virginia, West Virginia and Maryland (US) managed by the National Park Service
description_wiki: Harpers Ferry National Historical Park, originally Harpers Ferry National Monument, is located at the confluence of the Potomac and Shenandoah rivers in and around Harpers Ferry, West Virginia.

entity: Chesapeake and Ohio Canal National Historical Park
aliases: C & O Canal National Historic Park
C & O Canal National Historical Park
Chesapeake and Ohio Canal National Historical Park Historic District
description: National Historical Park located in the District of Columbia and the states of Maryland and West Virginia
description_wiki: The Chesapeake and Ohio Canal National Historical Park is located in the District of Columbia and the state of Maryland.
